ocr.text: In the context of Linde plc’s ongoing commitment to enhancing its operational efficiency and product reliability within the fine
chemicals sector, the analysis of batch yields serves as a critical performance indicator. The data presented in the accompanying
table illustrates the projected fine chemical batch yields for North America across multiple fiscal years, specifically highlighting
the anticipated yield for 2024 at 88.2%. This figure represents a notable benchmark against the yields recorded in 2023, thus

providing insight into the trends and targets set by the company. 

The emphasis on fine chemical production is particularly
pertinent in light of the increasing market demand for high-purity chemicals used in various applications, from pharmaceuticals
to specialty materials.
FineChemicalBatchYields  Year  TotalYield  YieldGrowth  YieldStatus  Category  Region
2023  NorthAmerica  85.7%  5.0%  OnTrack  FineChemicals  CAD  2022
2024  NorthAmerica  88.2%  3.2%  OnTrack  FineChemicals  CAD  2023  2025
NorthAmerica  90.5%  2.6%  OnTrack  FineChemicals  CAD  2024  2026  NorthAmerica
91.8%  1.4%  OnTrack  FineChemicals  CAD  2025  2027  NorthAmerica  93.0%
1.3%  OnTrack  FineChemicals  CAD  2026  2028  NorthAmerica  94.5%  1.6%
OnTrack  FineChemicals  CAD  2027  2029  NorthAmerica  95.0%  0.5%  OnTrack
FineChemicals  CAD  2028  2030  NorthAmerica  96.0%  1.0%  OnTrack  FineChemicals
As such, the metric reflecting the percentage yield not only serves as a measure of efficiency but also underscores Linde plc's
strategic focus on maintaining high-quality standards while optimizing production processes. In addition to yield percentages,
the table also outlines the percentage change in yields, which is projected to be 3.2% for the upcoming year, further illustrating

the company’s trajectory toward enhanced operational performance.

Moreover, the "OnTrack" designation included in the table
signifies that Linde plc is meeting its planned objectives for fine chemical production, ensuring that its processes align with the
overall strategic goals laid out for 2025. This proactive approach is crucial in a competitive landscape where companies must
continually innovate and adapt to meet evolving customer needs. The financial implications of these yield improvements are
further underscored by the conversion of yields into projected revenue, which is expressed in Canadian Dollars (CAD),

reflecting the company’s significant footprint in the North American market.

By closely monitoring these metrics, Linde plc not
only aims to improve its production capabilities but also to enhance its overall market position within the fine chemicals
industry. The insights derived from this data will aid in strategic decision-making processes, allowing the company to allocate
resources more effectively and respond to market demands with agility. The subsequent years will be critical for assessing the
validity of these projections and the overall efficacy of Linde plc's operational strategies in achieving sustainable growth within
the fine chemicals domain.
